items.title: Use the right solver for matrix structure
items.avoid: Calling generic `eig` on large symmetric or sparse matrices when specialized routines exist.
items.prefer: Use `eigh` for symmetric/Hermitian matrices; sparse `eigsh` for a few extremal eigenvalues on large sparse operators.
items.why: Structure-aware solvers are faster, more stable and return mathematically appropriate outputs (real spectra, orthogonality).
items.example: For a 10k-node graph Laplacian, `eigsh(L, k=5, which='SM')` computes five smallest eigenvalues instead of dense O(n³) `eig`.

items.title: Verify eigenpairs numerically
items.avoid: Trusting printed eigenvalues without checking Av = λv residuals.
items.prefer: Report ||Av - λv|| / ||v|| for each pair when debugging pipelines.
items.why: Ill-conditioned problems can produce misleading eigenvectors that fail the defining equation.
items.example: After `eig`, assert `np.allclose(A @ v, lam * v, rtol=1e-5)` before using v in downstream code.
